era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age. The Hyksos consolidate their rule in Egypt; Kassite power rises in Mesopotamia. Minoan Crete flourishes. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase but shows signs of stress. In China, the Xia dynasty (if historical) or early Shang period continues.

events.title: Continued Hyksos administration in the Nile Delta
events.description: The Hyksos maintain their administrative control over Lower Egypt and the Delta region. Archaeological evidence from Tell el-Dab'a (ancient Avaris) shows continued occupation and construction activity. The Hyksos adopt Egyptian administrative practices while maintaining their own military and cultural identity. Tribute and trade flow from Nubia and the Levant to the Hyksos-controlled north.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for this individual year. The statement reflects the general continuity of Hyksos rule during this period, based on archaeological stratigraphy.
cross_references: Part of broader Hyksos period (-1700 to -1550)

events.title: Minoan maritime trade networks active
events.description: Minoan traders continue to dominate Eastern Mediterranean commerce, exchanging pottery, textiles, and luxury goods with Egypt, the Levant, and the Aegean islands. Minoan pottery appears in Egyptian contexts, and Minoan merchants are depicted in Egyptian tomb paintings. This period represents the height of Minoan commercial influence before the later Mycenaean expansion.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence of pottery distribution and trade goods
Egyptian tomb paintings (18th Dynasty, later depictions of earlier trade)

events.certainty_note: The distribution of Minoan pottery and the evidence of trade networks are archaeologically well-established. The dating is based on stratigraphic analysis and pottery typology.
cross_references: Part of Late Minoan I period (-1700 to -1450)

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the assumption that Hyksos rule was uniform and stable across the Delta is questioned by some scholars, who suggest regional variation and possible coexistence with native Egyptian rulers in Upper Egypt.
historiographic_note: This year has no recorded specific events in surviving sources. The entries above represent inferred continuity based on archaeological evidence and broader historical patterns. The historical record for this year is essentially negligible in terms of documented events. Our knowledge comes entirely from material culture and later historical syntheses.

graph_edges.note: Minoan trade networks flourish during the Late Minoan I period
